explanation: Citrus aurantium dulcis (sweet orange) peel oil is a cold-pressed citrus essential oil with a bright, fresh scent but a high sensitization risk primarily due to its limonene content, which oxidizes on skin to form allergenic compounds. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly with repeated leave-on exposure.

Citrus grandis (grapefruit) peel oil is a cold-pressed citrus essential oil with a tart, fresh scent but significant sensitization risk due to its high limonene content, which oxidizes on skin to form allergenic compounds. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin with repeated leave-on use.

Citrus limon (lemon) fruit extract is a botanical antioxidant containing vitamin C, flavonoids, and hesperidin that provide free-radical-scavenging and mild brightening benefits. Unlike the essential oil, the fruit extract does not typically concentrate sensitizing limonene and is considered well-tolerated at typical formula concentrations.

Citrus limon (lemon) peel oil is a cold-pressed citrus essential oil with high limonene content that oxidizes on skin to form sensitizing compounds, and also contains phototoxic furanocoumarins — particularly bergapten — that can cause burns and lasting hyperpigmentation when skin is exposed to sunlight after application. It is a significant sensitizer and phototoxic risk in leave-on products, and is best avoided by reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin.

Citrus limon (lemon) seed oil is a cold-pressed oil from lemon seeds containing sensitizing compounds including limonene. It can trigger contact sensitization and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, and should be used with caution in leave-on products.

Citrus nobilis (mandarin) peel oil is a cold-pressed citrus essential oil with a sweet, warm scent but sensitization risk from its limonene content, which oxidizes on skin to form allergenic compounds. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly with repeated leave-on exposure.

Citrus reticulata (tangerine/mandarin) peel extract is a botanical antioxidant containing tangeretin, nobiletin, and vitamin C with free-radical-scavenging and mild brightening properties. Unlike the essential oil, the extract form does not concentrate sensitizing limonene and is considered well-tolerated at typical formula concentrations.

Citrus tangerina (tangerine) extract is a botanical antioxidant providing flavonoids and vitamin C with free-radical-scavenging and mild brightening benefits. As an extract (not essential oil), it does not concentrate sensitizing limonene and is considered well-tolerated by most skin types.

Citrus tangerina (tangerine) extract is a botanical antioxidant providing flavonoids and vitamin C with free-radical-scavenging and brightening properties. As an extract rather than an essential oil, it does not concentrate sensitizing limonene and is generally well-tolerated across skin types.

Citrus aurantium dulcis (sweet orange) peel oil — the asterisk indicates organic certification — is a cold-pressed citrus essential oil with a bright, fresh scent but a sensitization risk due to its high limonene content, which oxidizes on skin to form allergenic compounds. Organic certification does not reduce the limonene sensitization risk; this oil should be used with caution on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin.

CL 42090 (Blue 1 / Brilliant Blue FCF) is a synthetic triphenylmethane dye used to impart blue tones to cosmetics and personal care products. It is considered well-tolerated in topical applications at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Clitoria ternatea (butterfly pea) flower extract is rich in ternatins and anthocyanin antioxidants that give it a striking blue-purple color, used in skincare for antioxidant protection and as a natural colorant. It is well-tolerated and is used in brightening and protective formulas for its potent polyphenol content.

Cocamide DEA is a coconut oil-derived surfactant used as a foam booster and thickener in cleansers and shampoos. While widely used, it has raised concerns about the potential formation of nitrosamines (carcinogenic compounds) under certain conditions, and the EU has restricted its use; it is considered acceptable in rinse-off formulas at approved concentrations but is less favored in modern clean formulations.

Cocamide MEA is a coconut oil-derived surfactant used as a foam booster and thickener in cleansers and shampoos. It is considered a milder alternative to cocamide DEA with lower nitrosamine formation potential, and is well-tolerated in rinse-off formulas at typical use concentrations.

Cocamidopropyl betaine is an amphoteric surfactant derived from coconut oil and dimethylaminopropylamine, one of the most widely used mild cleansing agents in shampoos, face washes, and body cleansers. It reduces the irritation potential of primary surfactants, is compatible with all skin types including sensitive, and is generally well-tolerated — though a small subset of individuals with propylamine sensitivity may react to an impurity (DMAPA) in poorly purified grades.

Cocamidopropyl hydroxysultaine is a mild amphoteric surfactant derived from coconut oil, used as a foam booster and conditioning agent in gentle cleansers and shampoos. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is considered one of the gentler coconut-derived surfactants with low irritation potential.

Coccinia indica (ivy gourd) fruit extract is a tropical botanical antioxidant containing polyphenols and beta-carotene with free-radical-scavenging and mild brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant content.

Coco glucoside is a mild sugar-based surfactant derived from coconut fatty alcohols and glucose, used in gentle cleansers and foaming formulas for effective yet gentle cleansing. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin, biodegradable, and is widely used in natural and clean-formula cleansers as a sulfate-free alternative.

Coco-betaine is an amphoteric surfactant derived from coconut oil, used in cleansers and shampoos as a mild secondary surfactant and foam conditioner that reduces the harshness of primary surfactants. It is well-tolerated and suitable for sensitive skin in rinse-off formulas.

Coco-caprylate/caprate is a lightweight synthetic ester derived from coconut fatty acids and caprylic/capric acids, used as an emollient carrier with a dry, fast-absorbing skin feel and excellent compatibility with actives. It is non-comedogenic, well-tolerated across skin types, and is a popular alternative to silicones in clean and natural-positioned formulas.

Coco-glucoside is a mild sugar-based surfactant derived from coconut fatty alcohols and glucose, used in gentle cleansers and foaming formulas for effective yet gentle cleansing. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin, biodegradable, and widely used in natural and clean-formula cleansers as a sulfate-free alternative.

Cocoa butter (Theobroma cacao seed butter) is a rich, occlusive plant butter high in stearic, oleic, and palmitic fatty acids with excellent emollient and skin-softening properties. It is generally well-tolerated by most skin types, though it has moderate-to-high comedogenic potential and may not suit acne-prone or very oily skin when used at high concentrations.

Cocoa butter glyceryl esters are modified esters of cocoa butter fatty acids and glycerin, used as lightweight emollients that retain cocoa butter's skin-conditioning fatty acid profile with a less occlusive, more spreadable texture. They are generally well-tolerated and have lower comedogenic potential than raw cocoa butter.

Cocoa butter PEG-8 esters are PEG-modified cocoa butter derivatives used as emollients and emulsifiers that are more water-soluble and lighter in texture than raw cocoa butter. They are generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Coconut acid is the mixed fatty acid fraction derived from coconut oil, primarily lauric and myristic acids, used in cleansing formulas and emollients to provide conditioning and mild surfactant activity. It is generally well-tolerated, though the lauric acid component has moderate comedogenic potential on acne-prone skin at higher concentrations.

Cocos nucifera (coconut) fruit extract is derived from coconut fruit tissue and provides amino acids, minerals, and polyphenols with skin-conditioning and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in hydrating and protective formulas for its nourishing benefits.

Cocos nucifera (coconut) oil is one of the most widely used plant oils in skincare — a rich emollient high in lauric acid with antimicrobial and occlusive moisturizing properties. It is well-tolerated by dry, mature, and eczema-prone skin, but rates high on comedogenicity scales; individuals with acne-prone or congestion-prone skin frequently experience breakouts from products containing coconut oil at high concentrations.

Coffea arabica (coffee) seed extract is rich in caffeine and chlorogenic acid antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and mild vasoconstrictive properties that help reduce puffiness and support skin radiance. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening, de-puffing, and anti-aging formulas for its dual antioxidant and circulation-modulating activity.

Coffea arabica (coffee) seed extract provides caffeine and chlorogenic acid antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and mild de-puffing properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and anti-aging formulas for its antioxidant and circulation-supporting activity.

Coix lacryma-jobi ma-yuen (Job's tears) seed extract is a traditional East Asian botanical antioxidant used in Japanese and Korean skincare for its coixenolide content and polyphenol antioxidants with brightening and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated and used in whitening and calming formulas for its ability to inhibit melanin production and neutralize oxidative stress.
